steps.text: Identify the total signatures collected: 30 on Monday plus \(s\) on Tuesday, giving a total of \(30 + s\).
Apply the requirement: the student needs at least 190 signatures to run. "At least" means \(\ge\), so: \(30 + s \ge 190\), which is the same as \(s + 30 \ge 190\).

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: \(s + 30 \le 190\) — says the total is at most 190, meaning the student could stop at 189 signatures and still satisfy the inequality. But 189 is not enough to run. Eliminated.
  • B: \(s + 30 \ge 190\) — says the total is at least 190, guaranteeing the student has enough signatures. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: \(s - 30 \le 190\) — subtracts Monday's signatures instead of adding them. This does not represent the total collected. Eliminated.
  • D: \(s - 30 \ge 190\) — subtracts Monday's signatures and goes in the right direction, but the structure is wrong; it would require Tuesday alone to exceed 220. Eliminated.
